mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3 percentage points within 30 calendar days
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and lifts equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 15 basis points per month over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production capacity utilization reduces input bottlenecks and lowers wholesale inflation pressures, allowing equity margins to expand and supporting positive forward market returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistically significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (Pearson r < 0.10) across three full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt increase in the federal funds rate raises borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors reprice discount rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points over a 60-day window
failure_condition: The tightening signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observed episodes, or the Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports equity index appreciation over a weekly trading horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns decouple from GDP growth momentum, showing a rolling correlation below 0.15 over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and industrial output confirms broad-based economic acceleration, reducing recession risk premia and driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative quarterly forward returns in more than 50% of instances where both series show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts fall while retail sales rise indicates a shift from durable goods to services consumption, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as corporate capex cycles unwind.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% while RSAFS rises > 2% over 60 days
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 20 consecutive days following the divergence signal, or the forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward negative outcomes.
